reasoning: As of the forecast cutoff date of November 9, 2025, NATO Article 5 has not been invoked during the specified window (which began May 28, 2025). While 2025 has seen heightened tensions, including drone incursions into Poland and hybrid threats in the Baltics, these incidents have been managed under Article 4 or other de-escalatory frameworks. Historically, Article 5 has only been invoked once in NATO's history (after 9/11). Given that the majority of the risk window has passed without an incident and the threshold for invocation remains extremely high, the probability of a first-time-since-2001 invocation occurring in the remaining 52 days of 2025 is very low.
